name: inverse-CDF multinomial draw from a probability distribution

op.signature: token_ids[B] int32 = inverse_cdf_sample(probs[B,V] fp32, uniform_samples[B] fp32)

preconditions: uniform_samples are in [0, 1) and are an INPUT (the RNG is external to the kernel). This is the determinism contract: the same (probs, uniform_samples) always yields the same token_ids, so verify is bit-exact. Production code generates uniform_samples on the host with any RNG stream (philox, torch, numpy) and passes them in.
probs need not sum to 1 to machine precision; if uniform_samples[b] >= the row total (possible under fp rounding), the LAST valid index V-1 is returned.
the token is the LEFTMOST vocab index j whose cumulative probability first exceeds uniform_samples[b] (torch.searchsorted(cumsum, u, right=True), clamped to V-1)

current Triton lowering loads one full row per program (BLOCK_V=next_pow2(V)); the production 100k+ vocab path needs a staged streaming scan (the SAME documented limitation as temperature_softmax) and is a tuning follow-up, NOT a contract change
top-p / nucleus sampling is a SEPARATE op (needs a device sort); this op is plain inverse-CDF over the full distribution

numerics.notes: Token selection is INTEGER-EXACT: a different sampled token is abs_err >= 1 and fails any tolerance, so rtol=atol=0 (bit-exact). The inverse-CDF runs on a FIXED-POINT (int64) representation: q = probs * 2**30 (the scale is an exact fp32 power of two, so q is identical in every backend), then an INTEGER cumsum + searchsorted(right=True) threshold, clamped to V-1. Integer addition is associative and exact, so the prefix is order-INDEPENDENT -- a parallel scan (the device kernel) and a sequential scan (torch) give the same int64 cumsum bit-for-bit, and the cdf > u crossing lands at the same token in every backend. This is why the op is HONESTLY bit-exact: a floating cumsum genuinely diverges between scan orders at V~4096 (parallel != sequential for non-associative fp32) and flips tokens, which a naive float implementation hits; the integer representation removes that divergence entirely. The fixed-point scale (2**30 ~= 1e9 levels) exceeds fp32's ~7 sig digits, so it adds no quantization error beyond fp32's own precision. The stochasticity is OUTSIDE the contract (uniform_samples is an input), so this op is fully deterministic and verify-exact.
